item.title: Our Bacteria Are Talking. We’ve Just Begun to Understand What They’re Saying.
item.description: The human microbiome is essential to our health, but scientists still know very little about it. Two researchers set out to map this largely uncharted terrain.

item.title: I Think the Workmen at My Condo Are Being Exploited. What Should I Do?
item.description: Many of us suspect they are undocumented, and being underpaid.

item.title: World Cup Tourists Are Giving Americans Exactly What We Crave
item.description: TikTok de Tocqueville have descended on our Walmarts, Buc-ees and Bass Pro Shops. Our delight at their content says a lot about us.

item.title: Has There Ever Been a Better Time to Be a ‘Degenerate’?
item.description: It used to be a nasty expression of contempt. Now all sorts of people laughingly self-identify as degenerate.

item.title: How American Socialism Changed, and Stormed the Democratic Party
item.description: The movement was better at critiquing the system than reshaping it. But it has never had this much mainstream political power.

item.title: The Sorrow, Rage and Crazy Joy of the Knockout Rounds
item.description: For this phase of the competition, picture an automatic carwash made of cheese graters.

item.title: Can You Make a Bartender Prepare Off-Menu Soft Drinks?
item.description: A ruling on a restaurant etiquette dispute.

item.title: Mick Jagger Knows He May Have Played His Last Rolling Stones Show
item.description: The legendary rock star, now 82, on how fame, touring and aging have changed him.

item.title: My Aunt Pretends My Cousin Doesn’t Have a Drug Problem. Do I Have to Play Along?
item.description: I am sick of the charade we are expected to maintain around them.

item.title: Is My Wife’s Sister’s Husband Really My Brother-In-Law?
item.description: A ruling on the limits of familial bonds.

item.title: The Long-Running, Never-Ending Argument Over What Counts as ‘America’
item.description: The label applies to the country, but also two whole continents. Our claim over it is yet another way for us to annoy the entire hemisphere.

item.title: The Hunt for the Counterfeiter Trying to Make the Perfect Bill
item.description: On the trail of the world’s best counterfeit artists.

item.title: Some of Us Got Tired of Lionel Messi, but He Won Us Over. Again.
item.description: Has any seemingly overhyped phenomenon done a better job living up to the hype?

item.title: Is Venezuela a Colony Now? A Sovereign State? Modern Empires Rule in Ambiguity.
item.description: Occupation is a hassle. Large countries have many new ways to control small ones.
